text: It's a LOB system within logistics - Naming it would likely dox me, but there's a few known memory leaks for some functions (Primarily accounting) and our users don't shut down their systems. It was cheaper (until recently, because apparently we need AI videos of presidents scoring hockey goals and punching CA Hockey players) just to buy RAM and never have to hear the complaints. :)

text: If you are working on toy OSes just for learning, you'll want to do it in a VM anyways, so it doesn't really matter. text: Well, if it's a USB device, you can write the whole driver in userspace with libusb, which is what most people do with USB devices these days. It's more for things like if you want to write a new PCI-E device driver, or work on the scheduler (which is a popular OS class exercise).
